name: CRUD/client-id-handling
description: Conformance tests for server behaviour when the client supplies an id on POST, and when the id in the URL mismatches the id in the body on PUT.

variable.name: serverAssignedId
variable.description: The id the server actually assigned after ignoring the client-supplied id

test.name: server ignores client-supplied id and assigns its own
test.description: POST a Patient that contains id='client-supplied-id-fhir262'. The server must return 201 and assign a different id (FHIR R4 §2.1.0.2: the server is the authority on logical ids on POST).

operation.description: POST Patient with a client-supplied id

assert.description: Response body must be a Patient
Server must NOT echo the client-supplied id back as the assigned id
Server must have assigned a non-empty id

test.name: update with id mismatch returns 400
test.description: PUT /Patient/fhir262-url-id with a body that has id='fhir262-body-id'. The server must reject this with 400 Bad Request because the logical id in the URL and the body disagree (FHIR R4 §2.1.0.6).

operation.description: PUT to /Patient/fhir262-url-id with body id='fhir262-body-id' — ids deliberately disagree

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request when URL id and body id disagree

operation.description: Teardown: delete the patient created in the client-id test (if the server did assign an id)
